Title: A temperature-responsive post-transcriptional control of plant immunity

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and immunity against pathogens
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports CBP60g promoter activation; promoter binding is reduced at elevated temperature, contributing to lower SA-pathway output
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein abundance unchanged across temperatures; promoter recruitment reduced at elevated temperature
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MED16 binds the CBP60g promoter under SA/BTH-inducing conditions. 2. Elevated temperature reduces MED16 recruitment without reducing protein abundance. 3. This supports selective thermosensitive impairment of transcriptional machinery at the CBP60g locus.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as part of the thermosensitive promoter recruitment machinery rather than as the principal causal regulator chosen for rescue engineering

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and immunity against pathogens
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to CBP60g promoter-associated transcriptional machinery; binding is reduced at elevated temperature
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MED6 association with the CBP60g promoter decreases at elevated temperature. 2. Protein abundance remains stable, indicating selective recruitment defect rather than abundance change.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper experimentally validates its thermosensitive promoter recruitment at the key CBP60g locus

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mediator complex subunit with multiple intrinsically disordered regions
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for transient expression
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and immunity against pathogens
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-localizes with GBPL3 in GDAC condensates that are temperature sensitive
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression; confocal microscopy; protein disorder prediction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MED15 co-localizes with GBPL3 in nuclear GDACs. 2. These condensates appear/disappear together with temperature shifts, supporting a thermosensitive condensate mechanism.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mechanistically to illustrate condensate behavior; not shown as direct genetic rescue factor

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and immunity against pathogens
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heterologous expression restores induction of BnaICS1 and BnaPR1 at elevated temperature in rapeseed
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently or stably expressed transgene in rapeseed leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression; stable transgenics; RT-qPCR after Pst infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis CBP60g function transfers across species to rapeseed. 2. Its expression restores SA-associated marker gene induction at elevated temperature in rapeseed.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports translational engineering in crops for heat-resilient immunity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Separated here to distinguish source species Arabidopsis from applied species Brassica napus

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate for salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and immunity against pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pathogen-induced in leaves; suppressed at elevated temperature
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes salicylic acid accumulation, but constitutive ICS1 expression alone does not restore pathogen-induced SA or basal immunity at elevated temperature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS1 expression and SA accumulation are reduced at elevated temperature after pathogen infection. 2. ICS1 is a key SA biosynthetic gene, but 35S::ICS1 fails to rescue SA production or resistance at high temperature. 3. Therefore ICS1 expression is not the primary rate-limiting thermo-sensitive control point identified in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000 infection; BTH treatment; elevated temperature (28–30°C versus 23°C)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as the decisive thermo-sensitive rate-limiting step in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RT-qPCR; hormone profiling; disease assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: 35S::ICS1 causes highly elevated basal SA and reduced growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited alone for engineering heat-resilient SA immunity because overexpression does not restore resistance at elevated temperature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a comparator showing that boosting biosynthetic enzyme level is insufficient without restoring upstream transcriptional control

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; 35S::uORFsTBF1 translationally regulated construct; native promoter for GUS reporter
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and immunity against pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restores pathogen-induced salicylic acid accumulation and basal/ETI immunity at elevated temperature
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary thermo-sensitive rate-limiting regulator controlling temperature resilience of the SA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Salicylic acid biosynthesis and broader immune transcriptional network
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ICS1; EDS1; PAD4; PBS3; PR1; ALD1; RLP23; LYK5; MKK4
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of SA biosynthesis and defense genes; 35S::CBP60g restores their induction at elevated temperature
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GBPL3; MED16; MED6; RNA polymerase II
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pathogen infection; BTH/SA signaling; elevated temperature negatively affects promoter recruitment machinery
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pathogen- and BTH-induced in leaves; transcription strongly suppressed at elevated temperature
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; uORFsTBF1-mediated optimized expression; GUS reporter; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; hormone profiling; bacterial growth assays; ChIP-qPCR of upstream regulators
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Elevated temperature suppresses CBP60g transcription mainly at the promoter level. 2. Constitutive CBP60g expression restores pathogen-induced SA accumulation and resistance to virulent and ETI-eliciting Pst strains at elevated temperature. 3. CBP60g restores expression of multiple defense genes beyond ICS1, indicating master-regulator function. 4. Optimized pathogen-inducible translational control of CBP60g preserves immunity with minimal growth penalty.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000; Pst ΔhrcC; Pst DC3000(avrPphB); Pst DC3000(avrRps4); BTH; elevated temperature (28–30°C versus 23°C)
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: 35S::CBP60g delays flowering, but uORFsTBF1-CBP60g minimizes growth/developmental trade-offs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value for engineering temperature-resilient immunity; optimized expression strategies can preserve defense without major growth penalty
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also transiently and stably expressed in rapeseed to restore BnaICS1 and BnaPR1 induction at elevated temperature

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and immunity against pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restores pathogen-induced salicylic acid production and basal immunity at elevated temperature
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Functionally redundant with CBP60g and becomes rate-limiting for SA pathway output at elevated temperature
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Salicylic acid biosynthesis and immune gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ICS1 and overlapping SA-defense targets with CBP60g
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of ICS1 and defense genes required for SA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pathogen infection; SA/BTH signaling; elevated temperature reduces GBPL3 recruitment to SARD1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RT-qPCR; hormone profiling; disease assay; ChIP-qPCR of GBPL3 upstream recruitment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD1 expression is downregulated by elevated temperature. 2. GBPL3 recruitment to the SARD1 promoter is reduced at elevated temperature. 3. Constitutive SARD1 expression restores SA accumulation and resistance at elevated temperature. 4. SARD1 and CBP60g act redundantly as decisive regulators of thermosensitive SA immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000 infection; BTH treatment; elevated temperature (28°C versus 23°C)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target alongside CBP60g for improving temperature-resilient SA immunity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single cbp60g mutant remains temperature sensitive because SARD1 is functionally redundant

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Guanylate-binding protein-like GTPase with intrinsically disordered region; condensate-forming regulator
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for eGFP-GBPL3/overexpression constructs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and immunity against pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates CBP60g and SARD1 transcription, but its condensate formation and promoter recruitment are impaired by elevated temperature, reducing SA pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Temperature-sensitive upstream controller of the decisive CBP60g/SARD1 transcription step
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of SA defense regulators via GDAC condensates
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for SA-induced CBP60g expression; binds CBP60g and SARD1 promoters less at elevated temperature
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms GDAC condensates with Mediator and RNA polymerase II; co-localizes with MED15
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: SA; BTH; elevated temperature negatively affects GDAC formation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein abundance remains similar across temperatures; nuclear condensate number decreases at elevated temperature
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function analysis; overexpression; confocal imaging; ChIP-qPCR; immunoblot; subcellular fractionation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GBPL3 is required for CBP60g expression in response to SA. 2. Elevated temperature reduces the number of nuclear GDAC condensates and decreases GBPL3 binding to CBP60g and SARD1 promoters. 3. Total GBPL3 protein abundance is not reduced by elevated temperature, indicating thermosensitivity at condensate/promoter recruitment level. 4. GBPL3 overexpression alone does not restore CBP60g expression at elevated temperature.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Mechanistically informative but overexpression alone appears insufficient to rescue the trait
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Temperature effect is locus-selective: GBPL3 recruitment is reduced at CBP60g and SARD1 promoters but not NPR1
